assertions.expectation: Explains that GKE's maximum upgrade parallelism is approximately 20 nodes simultaneously, regardless of maxSurge setting
assertions.reason: The response suggests increasing maxSurge settings for faster upgrades but does not mention GKE's ~20 node parallelism limit or that maxSurge doesn't override this constraint

assertions.expectation: Calculates that at 20-node parallelism, upgrading 3,000 nodes requires ~150 batches — far more than an 8-hour window allows
assertions.reason: No mathematical calculation is provided showing the batch count or time requirements based on GKE's parallelism limitations

assertions.expectation: Recommends splitting upgrades across multiple maintenance windows (weekends) rather than attempting all at once
assertions.reason: The response recommends extending to a full weekend window and staggering upgrades by priority across separate windows

assertions.expectation: Suggests upgrading CPU pools and GPU pools separately, prioritizing CPU pools first as they're less disruptive
assertions.reason: The response explicitly recommends upgrading critical CPU pools first in separate windows, with GPU pools in subsequent windows

assertions.expectation: Recommends upgrading GPU pools during scheduled gaps between training/inference workloads
assertions.reason: While it mentions GPU pools are 'typically less critical for base services,' it doesn't specifically address scheduling around training/inference workload gaps

assertions.expectation: Suggests expanding the maintenance window or using multiple consecutive windows for large pool upgrades
assertions.reason: The response shows extending maintenance window to cover full weekend (Saturday 2am to Sunday 10am) and suggests multiple windows for different pool types

assertions.expectation: Mentions that cluster autoscaler can only scale one node pool at a time — this interacts with upgrade scheduling
assertions.reason: No mention of cluster autoscaler limitations or how they interact with upgrade scheduling

assertions.expectation: Addresses the 15,000 node standard limit and notes GKE 1.31+ supports up to 65,000 for AI workloads
assertions.reason: No mention of GKE node limits or the increased limits available for AI workloads in newer versions
